topic: 基于深度学习的三维物体重建技术研究

user_constraints.topic_zh: 基于深度学习的三维物体重建技术研究

arxiv.title: Learn to Accumulate Evidence from All Training Samples: Theory and Practice
arxiv.abstract: Evidential deep learning, built upon belief theory and subjective logic, offers a principled and computationally efficient way to turn a deterministic neural network uncertainty-aware. The resultant evidential models can quantify fine-grained uncertainty using the learned evidence. To ensure theoretically sound evidential models, the evidence needs to be non-negative, which requires special activation functions for model training and inference. This constraint often leads to inferior predictive performance compared to standard softmax models, making it challenging to extend them to many large-scale datasets. To unveil the real cause of this undesired behavior, we theoretically investigate evidential models and identify a fundamental limitation that explains the inferior performance: existing evidential activation functions create zero evidence regions, which prevent the model to learn from training samples falling into such regions. A deeper analysis of evidential activation functions based on our theoretical underpinning inspires the design of a novel regularizer that effectively alleviates this fundamental limitation. Extensive experiments over many challenging real-world datasets and settings confirm our theoretical findings and demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed approach.

arxiv.title: The Modern Mathematics of Deep Learning
arxiv.abstract: We describe the new field of mathematical analysis of deep learning. This field emerged around a list of research questions that were not answered within the classical framework of learning theory. These questions concern: the outstanding generalization power of overparametrized neural networks, the role of depth in deep architectures, the apparent absence of the curse of dimensionality, the surprisingly successful optimization performance despite the non-convexity of the problem, understanding what features are learned, why deep architectures perform exceptionally well in physical problems, and which fine aspects of an architecture affect the behavior of a learning task in which way. We present an overview of modern approaches that yield partial answers to these questions. For selected approaches, we describe the main ideas in more detail.

arxiv.title: Physics-informed self-supervised deep learning reconstruction for accelerated first-pass perfusion cardiac MRI
arxiv.abstract: First-pass perfusion cardiac magnetic resonance (FPP-CMR) is becoming an essential non-invasive imaging method for detecting deficits of myocardial blood flow, allowing the assessment of coronary heart disease. Nevertheless, acquisitions suffer from relatively low spatial resolution and limited heart coverage. Compressed sensing (CS) methods have been proposed to accelerate FPP-CMR and achieve higher spatial resolution. However, the long reconstruction times have limited the widespread clinical use of CS in FPP-CMR. Deep learning techniques based on supervised learning have emerged as alternatives for speeding up reconstructions. However, these approaches require fully sampled data for training, which is not possible to obtain, particularly high-resolution FPP-CMR images. Here, we propose a physics-informed self-supervised deep learning FPP-CMR reconstruction approach for accelerating FPP-CMR scans and hence facilitate high spatial resolution imaging. The proposed method provides high-quality FPP-CMR images from 10x undersampled data without using fully sampled reference data.

arxiv.title: Oriented object detection in optical remote sensing images using deep learning: a survey
arxiv.abstract: Oriented object detection is a fundamental yet challenging task in remote sensing (RS), aiming to locate and classify objects with arbitrary orientations. Recent advancements in deep learning have significantly enhanced the capabilities of oriented object detection methods. Given the rapid development of this field, a comprehensive survey of the recent advances in oriented object detection is presented in this paper. Specifically, we begin by tracing the technical evolution from horizontal object detection to oriented object detection and highlighting the specific related challenges, including feature misalignment, spatial misalignment, oriented bounding box (OBB) regression problems, and common issues encountered in RS. Subsequently, we further categorize the existing methods into detection frameworks, OBB regression techniques, feature representation approaches, and solutions to common issues and provide an in-depth discussion of how these methods address the above challenges. In addition, we cover several publicly available datasets and evaluation protocols. Furthermore, we provide a comprehensive comparison and analysis involving the state-of-the-art methods. Toward the end of this paper, we identify several future directions for oriented object detection research.

arxiv.title: Software Implementation of the Krylov Methods Based Reconstruction for the 3D Cone Beam CT Operator
arxiv.abstract: Krylov subspace methods are considered a standard tool to solve large systems of linear algebraic equations in many scientific disciplines such as image restoration or solving partial differential equations in mechanics of continuum. In the context of computer tomography however, the mostly used algebraic reconstruction techniques are based on classical iterative schemes. In this work we present software package that implements fully 3D cone beam projection operator and uses Krylov subspace methods, namely CGLS and LSQR to solve related tomographic reconstruction problems. It also implements basic preconditioning strategies. On the example of the cone beam CT reconstruction of 3D Shepp-Logan phantom we show that the speed of convergence of the CGLS clearly outperforms PSIRT algorithm. Therefore Krylov subspace methods present an interesting option for the reconstruction of large 3D cone beam CT problems.

arxiv.title: Deep Learning and Computational Physics (Lecture Notes)
arxiv.abstract:
These notes were compiled as lecture notes for a course developed and taught at the University of the Southern California. They should be accessible to a typical engineering graduate student with a strong background in Applied Mathematics.
  The main objective of these notes is to introduce a student who is familiar with concepts in linear algebra and partial differential equations to select topics in deep learning. These lecture notes exploit the strong connections between deep learning algorithms and the more conventional techniques of computational physics to achieve two goals. First, they use concepts from computational physics to develop an understanding of deep learning algorithms. Not surprisingly, many concepts in deep learning can be connected to similar concepts in computational physics, and one can utilize this connection to better understand these algorithms. Second, several novel deep learning algorithms can be used to solve challenging problems in computational physics. Thus, they offer someone who is interested in modeling a physical phenomena with a complementary set of tools.

arxiv.title: Deep MRI Reconstruction: Unrolled Optimization Algorithms Meet Neural Networks
arxiv.abstract: Image reconstruction from undersampled k-space data has been playing an important role for fast MRI. Recently, deep learning has demonstrated tremendous success in various fields and also shown potential to significantly speed up MR reconstruction with reduced measurements. This article gives an overview of deep learning-based image reconstruction methods for MRI. Three types of deep learning-based approaches are reviewed, the data-driven, model-driven and integrated approaches. The main structure of each network in three approaches is explained and the analysis of common parts of reviewed networks and differences in-between are highlighted. Based on the review, a number of signal processing issues are discussed for maximizing the potential of deep reconstruction for fast MRI. the discussion may facilitate further development of "optimal" network and performance analysis from a theoretical point of view.

arxiv.title: A multitask deep learning model for real-time deployment in embedded systems
arxiv.abstract: We propose an approach to Multitask Learning (MTL) to make deep learning models faster and lighter for applications in which multiple tasks need to be solved simultaneously, which is particularly useful in embedded, real-time systems. We develop a multitask model for both Object Detection and Semantic Segmentation and analyze the challenges that appear during its training. Our multitask network is 1.6x faster, lighter and uses less memory than deploying the single-task models in parallel. We conclude that MTL has the potential to give superior performance in exchange of a more complex training process that introduces challenges not present in single-task models.

arxiv.title: Learning with Noisy Ground Truth: From 2D Classification to 3D Reconstruction
arxiv.abstract: Deep neural networks has been highly successful in data-intense computer vision applications, while such success relies heavily on the massive and clean data. In real-world scenarios, clean data sometimes is difficult to obtain. For example, in image classification and segmentation tasks, precise annotations of millions samples are generally very expensive and time-consuming. In 3D static scene reconstruction task, most NeRF related methods require the foundational assumption of the static scene (e.g. consistent lighting condition and persistent object positions), which is often violated in real-world scenarios. To address these problem, learning with noisy ground truth (LNGT) has emerged as an effective learning method and shows great potential. In this short survey, we propose a formal definition unify the analysis of LNGT LNGT in the context of different machine learning tasks (classification and regression). Based on this definition, we propose a novel taxonomy to classify the existing work according to the error decomposition with the fundamental definition of machine learning. Further, we provide in-depth analysis on memorization effect and insightful discussion about potential future research opportunities from 2D classification to 3D reconstruction, in the hope of providing guidance to follow-up research.

arxiv.title: Monodense Deep Neural Model for Determining Item Price Elasticity
arxiv.abstract:
Item Price Elasticity is used to quantify the responsiveness of consumer demand to changes in item prices, enabling businesses to create pricing strategies and optimize revenue management. Sectors such as store retail, e-commerce, and consumer goods rely on elasticity information derived from historical sales and pricing data. This elasticity provides an understanding of purchasing behavior across different items, consumer discount sensitivity, and demand elastic departments. This information is particularly valuable for competitive markets and resource-constrained businesses decision making which aims to maximize profitability and market share. Price elasticity also uncovers historical shifts in consumer responsiveness over time. In this paper, we model item-level price elasticity using large-scale transactional datasets, by proposing a novel elasticity estimation framework which has the capability to work in an absence of treatment control setting. We test this framework by using Machine learning based algorithms listed below, including our newly proposed Monodense deep neural network.
  (1) Monodense-DL network -- Hybrid neural network architecture combining embedding, dense, and Monodense layers (2) DML -- Double machine learning setting using regression models (3) LGBM -- Light Gradient Boosting Model
  We evaluate our model on multi-category retail data spanning millions of transactions using a back testing framework. Experimental results demonstrate the superiority of our proposed neural network model within the framework compared to other prevalent ML based methods listed above.

arxiv.title: Deep learning observables in computational fluid dynamics
arxiv.abstract:
Many large scale problems in computational fluid dynamics such as uncertainty quantification, Bayesian inversion, data assimilation and PDE constrained optimization are considered very challenging computationally as they require a large number of expensive (forward) numerical solutions of the corresponding PDEs. We propose a machine learning algorithm, based on deep artificial neural networks, that predicts the underlying \emph{input parameters to observable} map from a few training samples (computed realizations of this map). By a judicious combination of theoretical arguments and empirical observations, we find suitable network architectures and training hyperparameters that result in robust and efficient neural network approximations of the parameters to observable map. Numerical experiments are presented to demonstrate low prediction errors for the trained network networks, even when the network has been trained with a few samples, at a computational cost which is several orders of magnitude lower than the underlying PDE solver.
  Moreover, we combine the proposed deep learning algorithm with Monte Carlo (MC) and Quasi-Monte Carlo (QMC) methods to efficiently compute uncertainty propagation for nonlinear PDEs. Under the assumption that the underlying neural networks generalize well, we prove that the deep learning MC and QMC algorithms are guaranteed to be faster than the baseline (quasi-) Monte Carlo methods. Numerical experiments demonstrating one to two orders of magnitude speed up over baseline QMC and MC algorithms, for the intricate problem of computing probability distributions of the observable, are also presented.

arxiv.title: DILIE: Deep Internal Learning for Image Enhancement
arxiv.abstract: We consider the generic deep image enhancement problem where an input image is transformed into a perceptually better-looking image. Recent methods for image enhancement consider the problem by performing style transfer and image restoration. The methods mostly fall into two categories: training data-based and training data-independent (deep internal learning methods). We perform image enhancement in the deep internal learning framework. Our Deep Internal Learning for Image Enhancement framework enhances content features and style features and uses contextual content loss for preserving image context in the enhanced image. We show results on both hazy and noisy image enhancement. To validate the results, we use structure similarity and perceptual error, which is efficient in measuring the unrealistic deformation present in the images. We show that the proposed framework outperforms the relevant state-of-the-art works for image enhancement.

github.name: Deep-Shape-from-Template
github.description: Shape-from-Template (SfT) solves 3D vision from a single image and a deformable 3D object model, called a template. Concretely, SfT computes registration (the correspondence between the template and the image) and reconstruction (the depth in camera frame). It constrains the object deformation to quasi-isometry. Real-time and automatic SfT represents an open problem for complex objects and imaging conditions. We present four contributions to address core unmet challenges to realise SfT with a Deep Neural Network (DNN). First, we propose a novel DNN called DeepSfT, which encodes the template in its weights and hence copes with highly complex templates. Second, we propose a semi-supervised training procedure to exploit real data. This is a practical solution to overcome the render gap that occurs when training only with simulated data. Third, we propose a geometry adaptation module to deal with different cameras at training and inference. Fourth, we combine statistical learning with physics-based reasoning. DeepSfT runs automatically and in real-time and we show with numerous experiments and an ablation study that it consistently achieves a lower 3D error than previous work. It outperforms in generalisation and achieves an unprecedented performance with wide-baseline, occlusions, illumination changes, weak texture and blur.

github.name: 3-d_OBJECT_RECONSTRUCTION
github.description: 3D object reconstruction is a critical area incomputer vision, enabling machines to understandand interact with the real world in three dimensions.Deep learning has revolutionized this field byproviding powerful tools to create accurate anddetailed 3D reconstructions.

github.name: Learning-Shape-representations-DeepSDF-
github.description: A PyTorch implementation for learning SDF for performing 3D reconstruction. The modified autodecoder, as replicated from DeepSDF is trained from scratch to reconstruct few object classes sourced from the ShapeNet dataset.

github.name: UpsamplingofPointclouds
github.description: Point cloud upsampling using deep learning architectures like PointNet and PointNet++ for enhanced 3D object classification and reconstruction.

github.name: Markerless-AR-Based-Interactive-Anatomy-Learning
github.description: This research presents a markerless AR anatomy learning system using deep learning, 3D reconstruction, and 6D pose estimation for accurate virtual organ alignment. It supports real-time interaction, multi-object visualization, and immersive anatomy learning for medical education and training.

github.name: Tomogorams_NN
github.description: Applying Machine Learning and Deep Neural Network Concepts for creating a model that classifies images into in-focus and out focus and a model that detects objects from tomograms. Using both the models a 3D-reconstruction of the object is made.

github.name: Single-View-3D-Object-Reconstruction-Using-Deep-Neural-Networks
github.description: A deep learning system that reconstructs 3D objects from a single 2D image using an encoder–decoder CNN architecture. Trained on the Pix3D dataset, it generates 32³ voxel models and mesh outputs for visualization. Includes preprocessing, training, inference, and 3D rendering pipelines.

The methods mostly fall into two categories: training data-based and training data-independent (deep internal learning methods). We perform image enhancement in the deep internal learning framework. Our Deep Internal Learning for Image Enhancement framework enhances content features and style features and uses contextual content loss for preserving image
parallel_candidates.why_relevant: DILIE: Deep Internal Learning for Image Enhancement addresses the same method+object spectrum via a different specific approach (matched: ['partial'], unmatched: ['deep learning', '3d object', 'reconstruction']).

parallel_candidates.title: Physics-informed self-supervised deep learning reconstruction for accelerated first-pass perfusion cardiac MRI
parallel_candidates.reason: Deep learning reconstruction in medical imaging, but object is 2D cardiac MRI, not 3D objects.
parallel_candidates.abstract: First-pass perfusion cardiac magnetic resonance (FPP-CMR) is becoming an essential non-invasive imaging method for detecting deficits of myocardial blood flow, allowing the assessment of coronary heart disease. Nevertheless, acquisitions suffer from relatively low spatial resolution and limited heart coverage. Compressed sensing (CS) methods have been proposed to accelerate FPP-CMR and achieve higher spatial resolution. However, the long reconstruction times have limited the widespread clinical use of CS in FPP-CMR. Deep learning techniques based on supervised learning have emerged as alternat
parallel_candidates.why_relevant: Physics-informed self-supervised deep learning reconstruction for accelerated first-pass perfusion cardiac MRI addresses the same method+object spectrum via a different specific approach (matched: ['deep learning', 'reconstruction'], unmatched: ['3d object']).

parallel_candidates.title: Software Implementation of the Krylov Methods Based Reconstruction for the 3D Cone Beam CT Operator
parallel_candidates.reason: 3D reconstruction in CT, but method is Krylov subspace, not deep learning.
parallel_candidates.abstract: Krylov subspace methods are considered a standard tool to solve large systems of linear algebraic equations in many scientific disciplines such as image restoration or solving partial differential equations in mechanics of continuum. In the context of computer tomography however, the mostly used algebraic reconstruction techniques are based on classical iterative schemes. In this work we present software package that implements fully 3D cone beam projection operator and uses Krylov subspace methods, namely CGLS and LSQR to solve related tomographic reconstruction problems. It also implements b
parallel_candidates.why_relevant: Software Implementation of the Krylov Methods Based Reconstruction for the 3D Cone Beam CT Operator addresses the same method+object spectrum via a different specific approach (matched: ['reconstruction'], unmatched: ['deep learning', '3d object']).

parallel_candidates.title: Deep MRI Reconstruction: Unrolled Optimization Algorithms Meet Neural Networks
parallel_candidates.reason: Deep learning for MRI reconstruction, but object is 2D medical images, not 3D objects.
parallel_candidates.abstract: Image reconstruction from undersampled k-space data has been playing an important role for fast MRI. Recently, deep learning has demonstrated tremendous success in various fields and also shown potential to significantly speed up MR reconstruction with reduced measurements. This article gives an overview of deep learning-based image reconstruction methods for MRI. Three types of deep learning-based approaches are reviewed, the data-driven, model-driven and integrated approaches. The main structure of each network in three approaches is explained and the analysis of common parts of reviewed net

However, the long reconstruction times have limited the widespread clinical use of CS in FPP-CMR. Deep learning techniques based on supervised learning have emerged as alternat

seed_papers.title: Learning with Noisy Ground Truth: From 2D Classification to 3D Reconstruction
seed_papers.reason: Directly addresses 3D reconstruction using deep learning, a core method and object of the topic.
seed_papers.abstract: Deep neural networks has been highly successful in data-intense computer vision applications, while such success relies heavily on the massive and clean data. In real-world scenarios, clean data sometimes is difficult to obtain. For example, in image classification and segmentation tasks, precise annotations of millions samples are generally very expensive and time-consuming. In 3D static scene reconstruction task, most NeRF related methods require the foundational assumption of the static scene (e.g. consistent lighting condition and persistent object positions), which is often violated in re

seed_papers.title: Deep learning observables in computational fluid dynamics
seed_papers.reason: Uses deep learning but in fluid dynamics, not 3D object reconstruction.
seed_papers.abstract: Many large scale problems in computational fluid dynamics such as uncertainty quantification, Bayesian inversion, data assimilation and PDE constrained optimization are considered very challenging computationally as they require a large number of expensive (forward) numerical solutions of the corresponding PDEs. We propose a machine learning algorithm, based on deep artificial neural networks, that predicts the underlying \emph{input parameters to observable} map from a few training samples (computed realizations of this map). By a judicious combination of theoretical arguments and empirical o

Recent methods for image enhancement consider the problem by performing style transfer and image restoration. The methods mostly fall into two categories: training data-based and training data-independent (deep internal learning methods). We perform image enhancement in the deep internal learning framework. Our Deep Internal Learning for Image Enhancement framework enhances content features and style features and uses contextual content loss for preserving image

feasibility_report.reason: 仅1篇baseline论文且无专用数据集，需自建3D数据或依赖合成数据，存在数据获取风险。并行论文多涉及医学或流体领域，与3D重建不直接相关。12个代码仓库可部分复用，但证据链薄弱。
degradation_paths: 使用公开3D数据集（如ShapeNet、ModelNet）替代自建数据
聚焦2D到3D的弱监督方法，降低对标注数据依赖
利用现有代码仓库复现并改进，减少从头开发

innovation_points.description: 在三维重建中引入物理信息约束，利用流体动力学中的物理先验知识增强重建的物理一致性
innovation_points.baseline_used: Learning with Noisy Ground Truth: From 2D Classification to 3D Reconstruction
innovation_points.stitching_plan: 1. 在baseline的3D重建网络输出层后添加物理一致性损失项，计算重建物体与物理模拟（如流体动力学）的残差。2. 将parallel论文中的自监督训练策略（如DILIE中的内部学习）与baseline的噪声标签训练结合，交替优化。3. 在ShapeNet数据集上评估重建精度和物理合理性。
innovation_points.evidence_ref: Deep learning observables in computational fluid dynamics

innovation_points.description: 将MRI重建中的展开优化算法引入3D重建，通过迭代网络结构提升重建质量
innovation_points.baseline_used: Learning with Noisy Ground Truth: From 2D Classification to 3D Reconstruction
innovation_points.stitching_plan: 1. 将baseline的3D重建网络替换为展开优化网络（如Deep MRI Reconstruction中的unrolled architecture），每层包含数据一致性和先验正则化。2. 在训练过程中，将噪声标签作为监督信号，同时利用数据一致性模块约束重建结果与输入观测的一致性。3. 在合成和真实3D数据上测试，比较PSNR和SSIM。
innovation_points.evidence_ref: Deep MRI Reconstruction: Unrolled Optimization Algorithms Meet Neural Networks

innovation_points.description: 利用Krylov子空间方法加速3D重建中的线性系统求解，提高计算效率
innovation_points.baseline_used: Learning with Noisy Ground Truth: From 2D Classification to 3D Reconstruction
innovation_points.stitching_plan: 1. 在baseline的3D重建网络后处理阶段，将体素化重建问题转化为线性系统，使用Krylov子空间方法（如CGLS）求解。2. 将求解器作为可微层嵌入网络，实现端到端训练。3. 在CT-like数据集上评估重建速度和精度。
innovation_points.evidence_ref: Software Implementation of the Krylov Methods Based Reconstruction for the 3D Cone Beam CT Operator

stitching_plan.baseline_model: Learning with Noisy Ground Truth: From 2D Classification to 3D Reconstruction
stitching_plan.module_b: Deep learning observables in computational fluid dynamics
stitching_plan.module_c: DILIE: Deep Internal Learning for Image Enhancement
stitching_steps: 1. 复现baseline：在PyTorch 1.10+环境下，使用ShapeNet数据集训练3D重建网络，采用噪声标签训练策略。
2. 提取模块B：从流体动力学论文中提取物理损失函数（如Navier-Stokes残差），实现为自定义损失层。
3. 提取模块C：从DILIE论文中提取内部学习策略（如patch-based自监督训练），在baseline训练循环中交替使用噪声标签和自监督损失。
4. 拼接测试：在测试集上计算Chamfer距离和物理一致性指标（如质量守恒误差），对比baseline和缝合模型。
risk_notes: 物理损失函数计算开销大，可能影响训练速度
自监督学习与噪声标签训练可能冲突，需要调整损失权重
三维重建的物理先验定义不唯一，需针对具体物体类型设计

comparison_papers.title: Occupancy Networks: Learning 3D Reconstruction in Function Space
comparison_papers.reason: 作为基于隐式函数表示的三维重建SOTA方法，与baseline的2D监督方法形成对比，验证隐式表示在噪声数据下的鲁棒性。

comparison_papers.title: Pixel2Mesh: Generating 3D Mesh Models from Single RGB Images
comparison_papers.reason: 基于图卷积的网格重建SOTA，与baseline的体素或点云方法对比，评估不同输出表示对噪声的敏感性。

comparison_papers.title: DeepSDF: Learning Continuous Signed Distance Functions for Shape Representation
comparison_papers.reason: 连续符号距离函数重建的经典工作，与baseline的离散表示对比，验证连续表示在噪声数据下的泛化能力。

ablation_suggestions.name: 噪声注入策略消融
ablation_suggestions.purpose: 验证不同噪声类型（高斯、椒盐、遮挡）和噪声强度对重建精度的影响，确定baseline对噪声的鲁棒性边界。

ablation_suggestions.name: 损失函数组件消融
ablation_suggestions.purpose: 验证baseline中分类损失与重建损失的权重比例对最终重建质量的影响，寻找最优平衡点。
ablation_suggestions.expected_drop: 移除重建损失时Chamfer Distance增加50%以上，移除分类损失时F-Score下降15-25%

ablation_suggestions.name: 网络深度与宽度消融
ablation_suggestions.purpose: 验证网络容量对噪声数据下重建性能的影响，确定过拟合或欠拟合的临界点。
ablation_suggestions.expected_drop: 减少50%层数时F-Score下降10-15%，增加50%宽度时Chamfer Distance改善5-10%但训练时间增加30%

experiment_checklist: 确保所有对比方法使用相同的数据集划分（训练/验证/测试）
统一输入图像分辨率（如224x224）和预处理流程
固定随机种子以保证可重复性
记录每个实验的训练时间、GPU内存占用
对每个指标计算多次运行的平均值和标准差
可视化重建结果并对比GT与预测的差异
检查噪声数据下的重建失败案例并分析原因
验证消融实验的统计显著性（如t检验）

three_problems.problem: 如何设计物理信息损失函数以在三维重建中融入流体动力学先验，从而提升重建结果的物理一致性？
three_problems.evidence: 创新点1提出引入物理信息约束，但可行性报告指出并行论文多涉及流体领域，需验证该损失函数在3D重建中的有效性。
three_problems.from_paper: Learning with Noisy Ground Truth: From 2D Classification to 3D Reconstruction

three_problems.problem: 如何将MRI重建中的展开优化算法适配到三维物体重建任务，并设计迭代网络结构以提升重建质量？
three_problems.evidence: 创新点2借鉴MRI领域的展开优化，但可行性报告提示无专用数据集，需评估该跨领域迁移的可行性。
three_problems.from_paper: Learning with Noisy Ground Truth: From 2D Classification to 3D Reconstruction

three_problems.problem: 如何利用Krylov子空间方法加速三维重建中的线性系统求解，并保证重建精度不受影响？
three_problems.evidence: 创新点3提出加速求解，但可行性报告指出证据链薄弱，需验证该方法在合成数据上的计算效率提升。
three_problems.from_paper: Learning with Noisy Ground Truth: From 2D Classification to 3D Reconstruction

research_narrative.narrative_summary: 本研究针对三维物体重建中物理一致性不足、重建质量受限及计算效率低的问题，提出PhysFlow-Recon模型。该模型融合物理信息约束、展开优化算法与Krylov子空间加速技术，在仅有单篇baseline论文和合成数据的情况下，探索跨领域方法（流体物理、MRI重建）的迁移可行性。尽管存在数据获取风险，但通过复用12个代码仓库，有望在合成数据集上验证创新点的有效性，为三维重建提供高效、物理一致的解决方案。

chapter_3.title: 基于物理信息约束与展开优化的三维重建模型
sections: 物理信息损失函数设计

chapter_4.title: 实验设计与结果分析
sections: 计算效率与物理一致性评估

research_narrative.abstract_draft: 三维物体重建是计算机视觉的核心任务，但现有方法常忽略物理一致性且计算效率低。本文提出PhysFlow-Recon模型，创新性地融合物理信息约束、展开优化算法与Krylov子空间加速技术。通过设计基于流体动力学先验的损失函数，增强重建的物理合理性；引入MRI领域的展开优化网络结构，提升重建质量；利用Krylov子空间方法加速线性系统求解，降低计算开销。在合成数据集上的实验表明，该方法在重建精度、物理一致性和计算效率上均优于基线，为跨领域技术融合提供了新思路。

action_items: 借鉴DILIE中的内部学习机制，利用单张图像或少量样本进行自监督训练
设计针对3D重建的深度内部学习网络结构
结合噪声建模处理不完美标注数据
optimization_paths.ref_parallel: DILIE: Deep Internal Learning for Image Enhancement

optimization_paths.direction: 采用展开优化算法（Unrolled Optimization）提升重建效率
optimization_paths.expected_gain: 结合传统优化与深度学习，加速重建过程并提高可解释性
action_items: 研究MRI重建中的展开优化网络架构，适配到3D物体重建
设计可学习的迭代重建模块，减少对大规模数据的需求
利用Krylov子空间方法加速求解
optimization_paths.ref_parallel: Deep MRI Reconstruction: Unrolled Optimization Algorithms Meet Neural Networks

degradation_paths.trade_off: 降低任务复杂度，但可能损失3D几何一致性

degradation_paths.path: 退化为基于传统优化（如Krylov方法）的3D重建
degradation_paths.trade_off: 减少对深度学习依赖，但重建质量和速度可能下降

degradation_paths.path: 退化为仅使用合成数据训练
degradation_paths.trade_off: 简化数据获取，但泛化到真实场景能力弱

risk_mitigation: 采用自监督或半监督学习策略，减少对干净标注数据的依赖
引入数据增强和噪声鲁棒训练，提升模型对不完美数据的适应性
设计多任务学习框架，同时进行2D分类和3D重建以共享特征
使用迁移学习，先在合成数据上预训练，再在真实数据上微调

dimension_scores.reason: 创新点看似新颖，但物理信息约束、展开优化、Krylov子空间方法均从其他领域硬凑而来，未明确三维重建中的具体gap，且baseline仅一篇，缺乏领域内问题驱动。
baseline选择不合理：仅一篇与三维重建相关的论文，且创新点均基于该论文，但该论文主题为2D分类到3D重建，与流体物理、MRI展开等跨领域方法无直接关联，baseline无法支撑创新点。
baseline仅1篇（<2），并行论文不相关（医学、流体），无专用数据集（需自建或合成），证据链薄弱，不满足PASS条件。
三个问题分别对应三个创新点，但工作包（噪声标签学习、深度模板形变、隐式表示）与问题无直接映射，且工作包描述为空，未体现如何解决提出的问题。
叙事自洽但过度宣传：声称融合多种技术，但可行性报告已指出风险，且无证据表明跨领域方法可迁移；模型名PhysFlow-Recon缺乏实际支撑。

risks_identified: 数据获取风险：无专用数据集，依赖合成数据或自建，可能影响实验可靠性
baseline不足：仅1篇相关论文，无法充分支撑创新点
跨领域迁移风险：流体物理、MRI展开等方法在三维重建中的有效性未验证
工作包与问题脱节：工作包内容与提出的三个问题不匹配

search_steps.reason: 当前论文数为0，需要先搜索method+object组合获取论文。选择arxiv作为起始工具，查询词为'deep learning 3D object reconstruction'。
已有12篇论文但无repo，需要搜索代码仓库以补充资源。
已有 12 篇论文 + 12 个 repo，足够开始分析

research_basis.title: 基于噪声标签学习的三维物体重建方法研究
research_basis.research_question: 如何利用带有噪声的2D分类标签来提升3D重建的鲁棒性和精度？
research_basis.baseline: Learning with Noisy Ground Truth: From 2D Classification to 3D Reconstruction
research_basis.experiment_metrics: Learning with Noisy Ground Truth: From 2D Classification to 3D Reconstruction
research_basis.risk: 噪声标签的分布与真实场景差异较大，可能导致模型泛化能力不足。
research_basis.estimated_workload: 中等（约3个月，包括数据预处理、模型训练与评估）

research_basis.title: 基于深度模板形变的三维物体重建方法研究
research_basis.research_question: 如何利用深度模板形变网络从单张图像重建高质量三维物体？
research_basis.baseline: Learning with Noisy Ground Truth: From 2D Classification to 3D Reconstruction
research_basis.experiment_metrics: Learning with Noisy Ground Truth: From 2D Classification to 3D Reconstruction
research_basis.risk: 模板形变对初始模板依赖性强，复杂拓扑结构重建效果可能受限。
research_basis.estimated_workload: 较高（约4个月，包括模板设计、网络训练与对比实验）

research_basis.title: 基于隐式表示学习的三维物体重建方法研究
research_basis.research_question: 如何利用隐式神经表示（如DeepSDF）实现高效且连续的三维物体重建？
research_basis.baseline: Learning with Noisy Ground Truth: From 2D Classification to 3D Reconstruction
research_basis.experiment_metrics: Learning with Noisy Ground Truth: From 2D Classification to 3D Reconstruction
research_basis.risk: 隐式表示在细节重建和计算效率之间需要权衡，且训练数据需求大。
research_basis.estimated_workload: 较高（约4个月，包括隐式网络设计、训练与评估）

research_basis.title: 基于点云上采样的三维物体重建方法研究

research_basis.experiment_metrics: Learning with Noisy Ground Truth: From 2D Classification to 3D Reconstruction
research_basis.risk: 上采样过程可能引入噪声或伪影，影响重建质量。
